Tests the deletion of a variable in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of the `delete` operator in JavaScript. It assigns the value `42` to a global variable `x` and then uses an `assert` statement to check if the `delete` operation on `x` returns `true`. The `delete` operator is used to remove a property from an object, and in this context, it is applied to a global variable, which is a property of the global object. The script tests the behavior of the `delete` operator when used on a non-configurable property in strict mode.
